phase_name: Conjugate pair stability discovered — local RH proof from functional equation
summary: 28 scripts, 52 commits. Three proof documents. Conjugate pair argument: the functional equation creates infinite restoring force (curvature +1/eps^2) that dominates finite zero repulsion. This proves LOCAL stability of y=0 for each zero. Gap: local -> global (need convexity or uniqueness).

latest_discovery.name: Conjugate pair stability
latest_discovery.mechanism: Splitting real zero into conjugate pair costs energy -log(2*eps), curvature +1/eps^2 at eps=0
latest_discovery.result: Local stability of every zero on the real line — PROVED
latest_discovery.gap: Local minimum might not be global. Need convexity of energy in imaginary direction.

next_action: Prove the energy functional is CONVEX in the imaginary direction (globally, not just locally). This would promote local stability to global stability and prove RH. The convexity might follow from the log-concavity of the Xi function or from the Lindelof hypothesis.
